rules.name: backward-compat-state
rules.evidence: `Controller._load_state` already tolerates a missing file, corrupt JSON, missing keys, unknown legacy keys, bad timestamps, and zones absent from the file (they seed fresh). Keep it that way. Adding a state field → add it to `_fresh_zone` with a safe default and read it with `.get(k)` / `is not None`; never assume it exists in an old file.

rules.name: backward-compat-entities
rules.evidence: Integration entity changes → additive. Renaming/removing an entity an old install relies on is breaking; avoid or migrate.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-f2-values
rules.evidence: F2-specific values are defaults/overrides, never hardcoded assumptions: entity ids (`switch.veg_main_pump`, `sensor.atlas_legacy_1_ec`, …)... A change that only works because of F2's exact names or numbers is a bug.

rules.name: deploy-rebuild-not-restart
rules.evidence: A plain restart re-runs the *baked* image (stale code); only a Rebuild re-COPYs `addons/f2_control/`... Never claim an engine change is live off a restart.

rules.name: commit-style
rules.evidence: Commit style: conventional commits (`feat:`/`fix:`/`docs:`/`chore:`) with a `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer when written via Claude Code.

rules.name: changelog-dual-view
rules.evidence: Changelog = dual view. Every release in `CHANGELOG.md` leads with 🌱 In plain English (anyone can follow it) then 🔧 Technical notes (entity/code detail). Keep both when adding a release.

rules.name: test-proof-required
rules.evidence: Any change to persisted state, add-on options, or entities needs a test proving an OLD install still loads.

uncompilable: What-this-is / architecture overview (HA integration vs f2-control add-on layers) — descriptive, not tied to an action
Phase logic P0-P3 semantics and grow-day/dryback definitions — domain knowledge, no tool-call binding
Hardware control sequence order (safety checks → prime → mainline → valve → irrigate → shutdown) — operational sequence, not regex-bindable to a specific edit
Key entity patterns naming conventions (crop_steering_<param>, per-zone zone_X, etc.) — naming convention too broad to scope without false positives
'Verify shot length from the live pump, not from deployed' — multi-step verification process, no single tool-call trigger
Shot sizing formula (dur = shot% × substrate_l ÷ flow_lps, per-plant vs zone-total units) — operator/HA-UI configuration guidance, not a repo code action
'One active branch: main. Retired branches kept as archive/* tags.' — descriptive of repo state, not a clear directive to block/require
Black reformatting exemption for addons/ in the lint command comment — explanatory aside on an already-scoped command, no clear violation condition
Historical note on the retired 'intelligence' layer (RootSense/ClimateSense) — informational only
Dependency notes (pure Python, no scipy/numpy in engine; voluptuous-only integration) — describes current state, not an explicit prohibition
